title: Reverse Engineering Enlightenment: Part 2
essence: Continuing the practical response to perennial problems, Vervaeke systematically presents psychotechnologies for addressing each: counteractive dynamical systems like the eightfold path combat parasitic processing; sati (mindfulness) restores being-mode and counters modal confusion; flow state dynamically integrates the reflectiveness gap; prajña (intuitive wisdom) overcomes perspectival clash and absurdity through non-dual scaling; inner dialogue via internalizing the sage addresses anxiety; communitas and authentic discourse cultivate connectedness against alienation; and gnosis rooted in higher states of consciousness addresses existential entrapment. All are framed within wisdom—a meta-virtue coordinating individual virtues. Enlightenment emerges not as supernatural attainment but as reliable, scientifically-grounded amelioration of perennial problems through integrated ecology of practices.
keyClaims: Counteractive dynamical systems (like eightfold path) address parasitic processing through interdependent, mutually-reinforcing practices.
Sati, flow, prajña, inner dialogue, communitas, and gnosis form an ecology of psychotechnologies targeting specific perennial problems.
Prajña achieves non-duality by simultaneously scaling maximally inward and outward, resolving perspectival clash.
Wisdom is the meta-virtue that coordinates cultivating and integrating all psychotechnologies for reliable optimization.
Enlightenment is definable, naturalizable, and achievable through disciplined cultivation of this ecology of practices.

concepts.name: counteractive dynamical system
concepts.summary: Interdependent set of practices that reinforce each other to counter parasitic cycles; exemplified by eightfold path.

concepts.name: sati
concepts.summary: Mindfulness practice remembering being-mode; access to participatory knowing beyond conceptual overlay.

concepts.name: flow
concepts.summary: State dynamically integrating immersion and reflective flexibility; resolves reflectiveness gap through optimal engagement.

concepts.name: prajña
concepts.summary: Intuitive wisdom state achieving non-duality through simultaneous maximal inward and outward scaling; resolves absurdity.

concepts.name: scientia intuitiva
concepts.summary: Spinoza's term for deeply intuitive knowing; interpenetration of individual thought with cosmic perspective.

concepts.name: non-duality
concepts.summary: State where maximal making-frame and breaking-frame are dynamically optimal; overcomes perspectival clash.

concepts.name: indwelling
concepts.summary: Polanyi's concept; practicing to see as the sage does, inhabiting another's perspective through serious play.

concepts.name: communitas
concepts.summary: Collective flow and genuine communion; shared attention and spirit creating distributed cognition and collective emergence.

concepts.name: authentic discourse
concepts.summary: Communication practice recovering real dialogue and understanding; addresses alienation through genuine relating.

concepts.name: gnosis
concepts.summary: Direct transformative knowing through ongoing mythos and higher states of consciousness; addresses existential entrapment.

people.name: J.D. Velleman
people.summary: On solving reflectiveness gap through Daoism and flow; dynamic integration rather than static balance.

people.name: Spinoza
people.summary: Scientia intuitiva and Ethics as extended meditation; recovering sacredness within Cartesian worldview.

people.name: D.T. Suzuki
people.summary: On prajña and simultaneous scaling up and down; perspective interpenetration in non-dual wisdom.

people.name: Meister Eckhart
people.summary: Neoplatonic Christian mystic; eye of seeing and being seen as reciprocal cosmic perspective.

people.name: Michael Polanyi
people.summary: Indwelling; proceeding through the tool toward participatory knowing.

people.name: L.A. Paul
people.summary: Transformative experience; cannot reason through epistemic gaps; need anagoge.

people.name: Agnes Callard
people.summary: Aspiration; overcoming existential ignorance through developmental process of becoming.

people.name: Victor Turner
people.summary: Communitas; unstructured community experiencing shared spirit and equality.

people.name: Jordan Hall
people.summary: Coherence; freeing communication from cultural grammar; distributed cognition and collective intelligence.

crossRefs.context: Previous episode identified perennial problems; this provides psychotechnologies to address each.
Next episode situates these practices within wisdom framing and 4E cognitive science worldview attunement.
Earlier neural basis of consciousness; small-world networks and flow states grounded in brain dynamics.

quotableMoments.quote: The goldsmith has to heat up the gold, shape it, and look at it—but not just one thing. Looking, heating, shaping—these are complementary. Together they produce wieldy gold that extends capacity.
quotableMoments.context: Analogy for ecology of psychotechnologies with complementary strengths and weaknesses.

quotableMoments.quote: You're simultaneously as deeply in and as deeply out, as deeply down and as deeply up as you can be. You're at maximal breaking frame and maximal making frame and they are optimally dynamically integrated.
quotableMoments.context: Non-dual state achieved through prajña; resolution of perspectival clash.

quotableMoments.quote: There is a collective that emerges there that takes your cognition and everybody else's in places that you can't go individually. You participate in that, but you don't make it.
quotableMoments.context: Communitas as distributed cognition exceeding individual capacity.
